storage_extensions.usage: mmap oky_nibble.bin (30MB, fits in 44MB L3). To probe orderkey ok: byte_idx=ok>>1, shift=(ok&1)<<2, nibble=(oky_nibble[byte_idx]>>shift)&0xF; 0xF=not_found, 0-6=year_offset(year-1992). Replaces the rebuilt 57MB orderkey_year static array — eliminates both the 57MB BSS memset (which caused ~14600 page faults single-threaded) and the 15M-row orders scan during build_joins. Preload into L3 with a parallel touch after mmap.
mmap price.bin (240MB vs 480MB for double). In revenue: revenue = price_i32[i] * (100 - disc_u8[i]) * 1e-4. Saves 240MB lineitem bandwidth.
mmap disc.bin (60MB vs 480MB for double). In revenue: factor = (100 - disc_u8[i]). Saves 420MB lineitem bandwidth.
mmap qty.bin (60MB vs 480MB for double). In cost: supplycost * qty_u8[i]. Saves 420MB lineitem bandwidth.

join_order.notes: Bitset[2000001] of qualifying p_partkeys (2MB, fully L1-resident during probe). Direct byte-test. Build parallel with 64 threads.
Probe nibble array: byte_idx=ok>>1, shift=(ok&1)<<2, yr_off=(oky_nibble[byte_idx]>>shift)&0xF. 30MB fits in 44MB L3. No rebuild cost — file persists in OS page cache between hot runs.
400KB direct array suppkey→nationkey. Fully L1/L2 resident. Single-threaded build (trivial).

join_order.build: partsupp(filtered by part_bitset, compact hash: cps_ht[1M slots])
join_order.notes: BSS static cps_ht[1M] × 16B = 16MB. DO NOT memset — BSS is already zero at program start, key==0 is empty sentinel. Parallel build with CAS for 440K qualifying partsupp rows. 16MB fits in L3.
25-entry string array indexed by n_nationkey (0-24). Load at startup.

scan.notes: Using compact column versions for l_extendedprice(int32), l_discount(uint8), l_quantity(uint8). Total: 14B/row vs 28B/row with doubles. Lineitem bandwidth ~840MB vs ~1680MB for these 6 cols (3 are kept as-is at 4B each).

data_structures.part_filter: bitset(uint8_t[2000001]) — 2MB, direct-indexed by p_partkey, set=1 if p_name contains 'green'. Built parallel.
data_structures.oky_nibble: mmap'd pre-built file (30MB) — nibble-packed uint8_t[30000002], orderkey->year_offset. 30MB < 44MB L3, fully L3-resident after parallel preload.
data_structures.cps_ht: static BSS compact_hash_map[1M slots × 16B = 16MB] — key=(int64 ps_partkey<<32|ps_suppkey), value=ps_supplycost(double). Sentinel key==0 (BSS zero). NO memset needed. splitmix64 hash + linear probe.
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local direct_array[25][8] of double — indexed by (nation_id, year_offset). 200 doubles per thread, register-resident. Merge after parallel scan.

preload_steps.action: Execute a trivial #pragma omp parallel num_threads(64) {} block before any timed phase. This pre-initializes the OMP thread pool, eliminating ~5-6ms of thread-spawn overhead from the first real parallel phase (dim_filter).
Read nation/n_nationkey.bin and nation/n_name varlen; populate string nation_names[25] indexed by n_nationkey.

mmap column_versions/orders.o_orderkey_to_year_nibble/oky_nibble.bin as PROT_READ MAP_SHARED. Then parallel-preload into L3: 64-thread loop reading every 64th byte to pull all 30MB pages through the cache hierarchy before main_scan begins.
Parallel 64-thread scan of part/p_name varlen (2M rows). memmem for 'green'. Set part_bitset[p_partkey]=1 for qualifying rows.
Single-threaded scan of supplier (100K rows). Write suppkey_nk[s_suppkey] = s_nationkey.

Parallel 64-thread scan of partsupp (8M rows). For each row where part_bitset[ps_partkey]=1: CAS-insert (int64_key, ps_supplycost) into BSS cps_ht[1M]. DO NOT memset cps_ht — BSS is already zero at process start, key==0 is the empty sentinel. This saves ~5ms of single-threaded page-fault overhead.

compute.year_extraction: From pre-built nibble file: yr_off = (oky_nibble[ok>>1] >> ((ok&1)<<2)) & 0xF; if (yr_off == 0xF) skip.
compute.amount_formula: double revenue = price_i32[i] * (int32_t)(100 - disc_u8[i]) * 1e-4; double amount = revenue - ps_supplycost * (double)qty_u8[i];
compute.note: Integer multiplication price_i32 * (100-disc_u8) avoids FP multiply for the revenue term. Cast to double at the end.

parallelism.parallel_phase: scan_lineitem_probe_join_aggregate — 64 threads, morsel_size=65536. Each thread holds local agg[25][8] doubles.
parallelism.notes: OMP warmup before any parallel work eliminates thread-spawn latency from dim_filter. cps_ht CAS inserts are low-contention (unique PK, 1M slots, 440K entries). oky_nibble preload (30MB parallel sequential read) fills L3 before main_scan random probes begin.

indexes.purpose: All lookups use pre-built in-memory structures or mmap'd column extensions

aggregation.thread_strategy: thread_local arrays (no atomics), final merge after parallel scan

optimization_notes: Root cause: two overlapping bottlenecks. (1) build_joins (63ms) was dominated by a 57MB single-threaded BSS memset for orderkey_year (~14,600 page faults, ~25ms) plus the 15M-orders scan; fixed via Category E — pre-building a persistent 30MB nibble-packed orderkey→year file (oky_nibble.bin) that lives in OS page cache across hot runs, eliminating the entire orderkey_year rebuild and memset. The 30MB nibble file also fits within the 44MB L3, making random probes L3-resident. The 16MB cps_ht memset is also eliminated (BSS is already zero at process start, key==0 is already the sentinel). (2) main_scan (35ms) was reading 2160MB of lineitem across 6 columns including 3 double columns (extendedprice, discount, quantity = 1440MB); fixed via Category C — using pre-existing compact column versions (l_extendedprice as int32 centiprice, l_discount as uint8 centesimal, l_quantity as uint8) reducing those 3 columns from 1440MB to 360MB, halving total lineitem bandwidth to ~840MB. Revenue formula adapts to: price_i32[i]*(100-disc_u8[i])*1e-4. (3) dim_filter's 16ms includes ~5-6ms OMP thread-pool spawn; fixed by adding a pre-warmup parallel region before timed phases. Combined effect: build_joins target ~10-15ms, main_scan target ~18ms, dim_filter target ~10ms, total target ~38-43ms.
